phrasal_verbs.meaning: stop talking; stop making a noise
talk more loudly so sb can hear you

A.title: Topic vocabulary (gap fill)
A.instructions: Complete the sentences with words from the box.

questions.sentence: The film was so ...... that everyone laughed all the way through.

Anger is a strong ...... that can be difficult to control.

B.title: Circle the correct word
B.instructions: Choose the correct option in each sentence.

questions.sentence: He was very **rude / romantic** to the waiter.
My little cousin can be very **naughty / shy** at home.
That joke was completely **ridiculous / polite**.
She is too **bad-tempered / enthusiastic** when she is tired.
The children were too **noisy / calm** in the library.
He has a great sense of **humour / stress** and makes us laugh.

D.title: Prepositional phrases
D.instructions: Complete each sentence with a phrase from the list.

questions.sentence: I didn't like the book ......, but now I love it.

...... his bad mood, he still helped us.

E.title: Word patterns
E.instructions: Write one preposition in each gap.

questions.sentence: She felt ashamed ...... her rude comment.

He was frightened ...... the dark.

F.title: Word formation
F.instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the word in capitals.

questions.sentence: That film was really ...... and everyone enjoyed it. (BORE)
